input: Conduct a comprehensive safety review of atorvastatin. Focus on hepatotoxicity signal, muscle-related AEs, and diabetes risk.

expected_details.hepatotoxicity: transaminase elevations (1-3%); serious hepatotoxicity rare; routine LFT monitoring no longer universally recommended
expected_details.diabetes_risk: small increased risk of new-onset diabetes (OR ~1.1-1.2); benefits outweigh risk in CV prevention

input: Provide a comprehensive immune-related adverse event (irAE) profile for pembrolizumab.

expected_details.management_principle: grade-based: hold for grade 2, discontinue for grade 3-4, high-dose corticosteroids
expected_details.monitoring: baseline TSH, LFTs, renal function; repeat Q cycle

input: Conduct a class-level safety review of NSAIDs for cardiovascular risk. Compare COX-2 selective vs non-selective.

expected_details.cv_risk_mechanism: COX-2 inhibition → prostacyclin/thromboxane imbalance → pro-thrombotic state
expected_details.naproxen_exception: may have lower CV risk due to sustained COX-1 inhibition (antiplatelet effect)
expected_details.fda_boxed_warning: class-wide CV risk warning on all NSAIDs (2005, strengthened 2015)

relative_risk.celecoxib: similar to nonselective NSAIDs at standard doses (PRECISION trial)
relative_risk.diclofenac: highest CV risk among non-selective NSAIDs

input: Provide a comprehensive safety profile for ciprofloxacin covering all FDA-warned adverse effects.

expected_details.prescribing_restriction: FDA recommends against use for uncomplicated infections when alternatives exist

input: Conduct a benefit-risk assessment for long-term opioid therapy (oxycodone) in chronic non-cancer pain.

expected_details.benefits: moderate short-term pain reduction; limited evidence for long-term benefit
expected_details.benefit_risk_assessment: risks generally outweigh benefits for long-term use in chronic non-cancer pain
expected_details.guidelines: CDC 2022 guideline recommends non-opioid therapies first; lowest effective dose if opioids used; regular reassessment
expected_details.rems: class-wide opioid REMS (patient education, prescriber training)

input: Review the safety profile of SSRIs in pediatric patients (ages 6-17), focusing on the suicidality boxed warning.

expected_details.boxed_warning: increased risk of suicidal thinking and behavior in children, adolescents, and young adults (18-24)
expected_details.evidence_basis: FDA meta-analysis of 24 trials: 4% suicidality in drug group vs 2% in placebo (no completed suicides)
expected_details.clinical_paradox: untreated depression also increases suicide risk; black box may deter appropriate treatment
expected_details.monitoring_requirement: weekly visits for first 4 weeks, biweekly for next 4 weeks

input: Document the pharmacovigilance timeline for the withdrawal of valdecoxib (Bextra). What safety signals led to withdrawal?

safety_signals: serious skin reactions (Stevens-Johnson syndrome, toxic epidermal necrolysis)
increased CV risk in CABG patients
expected_details.regulatory_action: FDA requested voluntary withdrawal; manufacturer (Pfizer) complied
expected_details.context: withdrew in wake of rofecoxib (Vioxx) withdrawal; valdecoxib had additional skin toxicity concern

input: Assess the safety of valproic acid in pregnancy. What is the risk classification and what are the alternatives?

expected_details.teratogenicity: neural tube defects (spina bifida) in 1-2% of exposed pregnancies (10x baseline risk)
other_risks: reduced IQ in exposed children (NEAD study)
fda_actions: mandatory pregnancy testing before prescribing to women of childbearing potential

alternatives.bipolar: lithium (lower risk but still monitored), lamotrigine

input: A large insurance claims database study (N=500,000) shows a statistically significant association between PPI use and dementia risk (HR 1.44, 95% CI 1.36-1.52). Evaluate this pharmacovigilance signal.

expected_details.signal_assessment: potential signal requiring further investigation but confounding likely
key_concerns: confounding by indication (GERD ↔ H. pylori ↔ dementia?)
protopathic bias (PPIs for prodromal GI symptoms of dementia)
expected_details.supporting_evidence: some mechanistic plausibility (vitamin B12 deficiency, amyloid interaction)
expected_details.contradicting_evidence: large RCTs (ASPREE) have not confirmed association; European cohorts show null results
expected_details.assessment: likely confounded; not sufficient to change prescribing practice without RCT confirmation

input: Outline the structure and requirements for a Periodic Safety Update Report (PSUR/PBRER) for a newly approved oncology biologic. What are the key sections and timeline?

expected_details.timeline: every 6 months for first 2 years, annually for next 2 years, then every 3 years
expected_details.data_lock_point: international birth date (IBD) of the product
